title: Tillaea androsace
taxonomy: Tillaea androsace belongs to the family Haloragaceae. It is a species of aquatic plant. Tillaea androsace is native to Australia and New Zealand. This plant is a perennial and can be found growing in wetlands and aquatic environments. The classification of Tillaea androsace is further divided into its kingdom as Plantae.
uses: Tillaea androsace is a species of flowering plant known for its potential medicinal properties. According to some sources, it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. Further research is needed to fully understand and confirm the medicinal uses of Tillaea androsace.
